source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Users can get their Kerosene stuck until TVL becomes greater than Dyad’s supply
summary: The protocol expects users to migrate their collateral from V1 vaults to V2 vaults, this significantly increases the TVL of the protocol’s V2. At the same time, the Kerosene price depends on the TVL, in UnboundedKerosineVault::assetPrice the numerator of the equation is:
root_cause: exit-path accounting or integration failure in Users can get their Kerosene stuck until TVL becomes greater than Dyad’s supply, allowing assets to become stuck in the protocol
broken_invariants: the vault path must not allow the condition described by "Users can get their Kerosene stuck until TVL becomes greater than Dyad’s supply" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: The protocol expects users to migrate their collateral from V1 vaults to V2 vaults, this significantly increases the TVL of the protocol’s V2. At the same time, the Kerosene price depends on the TVL, in UnboundedKerosineVault::assetPrice the numerator of the equation is:

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: This is a bit tricky, but I think the most straightforward and logical solution would be to block the usage of the Kerosene vaults (just keep them unlicensed) until enough users migrate their positions from V1, i.e. the TVL reaches the Dyad’s total supply.
